sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.71 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: Let A be a finite group with a normal subgroup$H$. A subgroup$U$of$H$is called an$A$-covering subgroup\/ of$H$if$_{a A} U^a=H$. Is there a function$f\!: { N} { N}$such that whenever$U< H < A$, where$A$is a finite group,$H$is a normal subgroup of$A$of index$n$, and$U$is an$A$-covering subgroup of$H$, the index$|H:U| f(n)$? (We have shown that the answer is ``yes'' if$U$is a maximal subgroup of$H$.)...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
